food_subgroup: Paneer dishes / Indian cottage cheese preparations

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for Kadai Paneer; closest match is a generic cottage cheese dish node. Kadai Paneer is a spiced Indian preparation of paneer with bell peppers in tomato-onion gravy.
ontology.foodex2_description: Paneer (Indian cottage cheese) — used as the primary ingredient anchor; no composite Kadai Paneer code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific composite code for Kadai Paneer. A0CKP (paneer) is used as the closest defensible ingredient-level anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Kadai Paneer as a prepared dish.

main_ingredients: kadai masala (toasted coriander seeds, dried red chilies)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant or home serving of Kadai Paneer is approximately 300 grams, typically served in a bowl or kadai alongside roti, naan, or rice.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Kadai Paneer is a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods using paneer, bell peppers, tomatoes, onions, and cooking oil as primary ingredient references. Values represent a typical restaurant-style preparation. Core macros (energy 160 kcal, protein 7.33g, fat 11.67g, carbohydrate 6.67g per 100g) are consistent with ICMR-NIN paneer-based dish profiles. Cholesterol, saturated fat, and trans fat values cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for paneer and dairy fat components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database analogous food reference for paneer-based dishes
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kadai Paneer in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 30 is estimated based on analogous low-GI foods: paneer (GI ~27, high protein and fat content suppresses glycemic response), bell peppers (GI ~15), and tomato-onion base (GI ~15–35). The high fat (11.67g/100g) and protein (7.33g/100g) content of the dish further moderates postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 300g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (30 × 20.01) / 100 ≈ 6.0. Estimation is consistent with ICMR-NIN guidance on paneer-based dishes.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (250 mg/100g) and variable oil/ghee quantity in preparation
hypertension.guidance: Kadai Paneer contains 250 mg sodium per 100g, which can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium intake at a 300g serving (approximately 750 mg); individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt and minimal added ghee, and pair with low-sodium accompaniments such as plain roti rather than salted breads.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~30) driven by high protein and fat content from paneer
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 30 and only 6.67g carbohydrate per 100g, Kadai Paneer has a low glycemic load and is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, portion control is important when served alongside high-GI breads such as naan or white rice, which can substantially raise the overall meal glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (6.67g/100g) from paneer and cooking fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kadai Paneer provides 6.67g saturated fat per 100g, which at a 300g serving delivers approximately 20g saturated fat — exceeding the recommended daily limit for individuals with cardiovascular disease; preparation with minimal oil and reduced-fat paneer, and limiting serving size to 150–200g, is advisable for those managing cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, coriander) and antioxidant-rich bell peppers
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The kadai masala spice blend, particularly turmeric and freshly ground coriander, contains bioactive compounds with documented anti-inflammatory properties; bell peppers contribute vitamin C (13.33 mg/100g) which supports immune function, though individuals with dairy sensitivity should monitor their response to paneer.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kadai Paneer is a popular North Indian main dish consisting of cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) and crunchy bell peppers (capsicum) cooked in a robust, semi-dry tomato-onion gravy. The dish is named after the 'kadai' (Indian wok or karahi) in which it is traditionally prepared over high heat. Its defining characteristic is the 'Kadai Masala' — a freshly ground spice blend of toasted coriander seeds and dried red chilies — which gives the dish its distinctive peppery, aromatic flavour. Unlike creamier paneer preparations such as Palak Paneer or Butter Paneer, Kadai Paneer is semi-dry with a chunky texture from sautéed onions and capsicum. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides approximately 160 kcal, 7.33g protein, 11.67g fat (of which 6.67g is saturated), and 6.67g carbohydrate, making it a moderate-calorie, high-protein, high-fat dish. The low carbohydrate content and high protein-fat ratio result in a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 30, making it suitable for individuals managing blood sugar. Calcium content is notable at 116.67 mg/100g from paneer. The dish is vegetarian, gluten-free, and halal-suitable. It is a staple of Punjabi cuisine and widely served in Indian restaurants globally, typically paired with naan, roti, or basmati rice. Clinically, the sodium content (250 mg/100g) and saturated fat (6.67g/100g) warrant attention for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Kadai Paneer?
Is Kadai Paneer good for diabetics?

Can I eat Kadai Paneer if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Kadai Paneer is a composite dish not directly listed as a single entry in ICMR-NIN tables. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN paneer and vegetable component data, consistent with the input enriched_json values which carry 0.9 confidence for most macros. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database references for paneer and low-GI vegetables. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Kadai Paneer as a composite dish is not available in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2024. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this composite preparation is not reported in available approved sources. 3. trans_fat_g (0.17g/100g) is retained from the input enriched_json; this is plausible for a dairy-fat-containing dish (paneer and ghee contain naturally occurring trans fats) and is consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for paneer. 4. Cholesterol (26.67 mg/100g) and saturated fat (6.67g/100g) values are cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for paneer (FDC ID 173420). 5. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Kadai Paneer exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2024. 6. Glycemic load per 300g serving = (30 × 20.01) / 100 ≈ 6.0 (low GL). 7. Portions in the input contained multiple duplicates and conflicting is_default flags; the canonical serving size of 300g (1 bowl) has been selected as the default based on the most frequently cited grabfood portion. 8. Vitamin A value (45.0 mcg RAE/100g) is expressed as mcg RAE as per RESULT template convention; the input value of 45.0 mcg is assumed to represent RAE given the food composition context.
